title: Steve Kerr explains how the Celtics went from an ‘afterthought’ to a ‘real favorite’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Steve Kerr praises Celtics' transformation into favorites despite early season doubts due to key player absences.
Celtics achieved growth by giving younger players experience in Jayson Tatum's absence, enhancing team depth.
Boston, second in the Eastern Conference, excels in spacing and offensive efficiency under coach Joe Mazzulla.

title: Advocates sue Worcester over response to mental health 911 calls, alleging discrimination
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Advocates filed a federal lawsuit against Worcester, alleging discrimination in 911 responses to mental health crises.
The lawsuit claims Worcester sends armed police officers to all mental health calls, worsening situations for individuals in crisis.
Fewer than 25% of Worcester police officers have completed crisis intervention training, impacting their response to mental health emergencies.

title: Hilary Duff will be Northeastern University’s 2026 commencement speaker
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Hilary Duff will be the 2026 commencement speaker at Northeastern University.
Northeastern's 2026 commencement will be held on April 29 at Fenway Park.
Duff is known for her roles in 'Lizzy Maguire' and 'A Cinderella Story'.

title: 3 roster needs Patriots must still address after free-agency spending spree
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots are seeking a proven No. 1 wide receiver, eyeing A.J. Brown as a potential trade target.
New England aims to bolster its pass rush, potentially through the draft, after K'Lavon Chaisson's departure.
The team is looking to add depth at offensive tackle, considering Clemson's Blake Miller in the draft.

title: Needham 16-year-old charged with murder in Roxbury slaying of woman, 61
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A 16-year-old from Needham faces murder, aggravated assault, and firearm charges in Roxbury shooting of Marie Molea-Anthony.
Police charged the teen for the fatal October 13 shooting on Washington Street in Roxbury, where Molea-Anthony was killed.
The accused was previously in custody on unrelated firearms charges and will be arraigned in Boston District Court.

title: Chelsea man released decades after murder conviction won’t be retried, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Thomas Rosa Jr., convicted in 1993 for a 1985 murder, was released after 34 years due to new DNA evidence.
The Suffolk County DA's Office dropped charges against Rosa, citing lost evidence, new forensic tests, and changes in case law.
Rosa's exoneration marks him as the 100th person in Massachusetts to be exonerated since 1989, according to his attorneys.

title: Former Attleboro man gets 35-40 years in prison for raping 5-year-old niece
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: William Machado, 35, was sentenced to 35-40 years for raping a 5-year-old in Fall River Superior Court.
Machado was convicted of multiple charges including child rape by force and indecent assault on a person under 14.
He was arrested in Chicago after being located through financial transactions and extradited to Massachusetts.

title: A month after tragic shooting, Colin Dorgan, Blackstone Valley hockey triumph to win R.I. state title
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Blackstone Valley hockey team won the Rhode Island Division II state title by defeating Lincoln High School 3-2 in quadruple overtime.
Colin Dorgan scored a key goal to tie the game in regulation, following a recent family tragedy affecting him personally.
The team wore jerseys with heart emblems to honor the victims of a shooting involving Dorgan's family during Senior Night.

title: Bruins call up former 1st-round pick Lukas Reichel on emergency basis
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins recalled Lukas Reichel from Providence on an emergency basis before facing the Winnipeg Jets.
Reichel, a 2020 first-round pick, was acquired from the Vancouver Canucks for a 2026 sixth-round draft pick.
In three games with Providence, Reichel scored one goal and added four assists before being called up.

title: World Cup games at Gillette no longer in jeopardy after Foxborough officially approves license
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough Select Board approved a license for seven World Cup games at Gillette Stadium, securing the stadium as a host site.
The agreement follows a $7.8 million security funding deal involving Boston Soccer 2026 and Kraft Sports & Entertainment Group.
The license includes a traffic plan approved by the Massachusetts Department of Transportation and enhanced MBTA service.
